name: Change, INC
description_short: CHANGE, Inc. is a non-profit community action agency in northern West Virginia and Ohio providing free health, housing counseling, and supportive services to low-income families.
description_long:
CHANGE, Inc. was established in 1983 by the Weirton Ministerial Association to support workers displaced by the closure of National Steel's Weirton Plant. Beginning with a $25,000 Community Services Block Grant, the organization expanded from a Limited Purpose Agency to a full Community Action Agency in 1990. In 2007, CHANGE, Inc. achieved Federally Qualified Health Center status, enabling it to serve Jefferson County and expand its reach across northern West Virginia and surrounding Ohio communities. Over 40 years, the organization has built a comprehensive service model addressing poverty, health disparities, and housing instability.

CHANGE, Inc. offers a broad range of services across four core areas: Health services include family medicine, gynecology, pediatrics, behavioral health, addiction management, and dental care through community health centers, school-based clinics, and a family pharmacy. Housing services encompass counseling, budgeting assistance, foreclosure guidance, down payment and closing cost assistance, weatherization programs, and home repair services. Advocacy programs support children and families experiencing abuse, neglect, or domestic violence through the Comfort House Child Advocacy Center and domestic violence programs. Supportive services provide assistance with food, housing stability, and income management.

CHANGE, Inc. distinguishes itself as a federally qualified health center with integrated services—combining medical care, housing counseling, and poverty-intervention programs under one organization. The agency serves regardless of income level, insurance status, or residence, reflecting its mission-driven approach. Leadership under CEO Lisa Mowry emphasizes values of integrity, compassion, collaboration, and empowerment. The organization maintains multiple locations (Weirton administrative office and Moundsville outreach office) to serve its five-county service area.

CHANGE, Inc. is genuinely a non-profit community resource rather than a consumer finance company. It offers legitimate free housing counseling and supportive services funded through grants and donations. However, consumers should note that as a community health center, services may have waitlists, eligibility requirements based on income, and limited hours. The organization excels at comprehensive case management but is not a substitute for professional credit repair or debt relief services.
pros: Federally Qualified Health Center status ensures quality care standards and comprehensive integration of health and housing services
Free housing counseling including budgeting, foreclosure assistance, and down payment/closing cost help
Serves regardless of income, insurance status, or residence—no barriers to access
Over 40 years of organizational stability and community trust in the Ohio Valley region
Integrated supportive services addressing root causes of poverty beyond single-issue solutions
Behavioral health and addiction management services available on-site
Multiple office locations (Weirton and Moundsville) for geographic accessibility
Board-led governance with published annual reports and community needs assessments showing transparency
cons: Limited geographic service area (5 counties in WV and OH only)—not available to consumers outside northern Appalachia
As a community action agency, likely subject to funding constraints and potential waitlists during high-demand periods
Does not offer credit repair, debt settlement, or credit monitoring—focused on prevention and stabilization rather than remediation
No online application portal explicitly mentioned for housing counseling or supportive services on website
Limited information about response times or service availability for housing counseling certification
best_for: Low-income families in northern West Virginia or southern Ohio seeking free housing counseling and foreclosure prevention
Individuals needing integrated health and social services (behavioral health, addiction treatment, food assistance)
Families experiencing domestic violence or child abuse needing advocacy and support
Homeowners seeking weatherization assistance, home repair, or down payment help in the Ohio Valley
diagnosis: CHANGE, Inc. is best for low-income individuals and families in northern West Virginia or southern Ohio who need integrated community support combining health services, housing counseling, and poverty-intervention programs. The main caveat is that this is a non-profit community agency focused on stabilization and prevention, not credit repair or debt relief—consumers with credit report issues or active debt problems should seek specialized credit counseling services elsewhere.
services: Family medicine and primary health care
Behavioral health and mental health counseling
Addiction management and substance abuse treatment
Down payment and closing cost assistance
Domestic violence support and advocacy programs
Child advocacy services through Comfort House
Food assistance and income stability support
School-based health centers for pediatric care
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
meta_description: CHANGE, Inc. is a non-profit community action agency in West Virginia and Ohio providing free health, housing counseling, and supportive services.
